findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md mandates 'All persistence MUST go through the Repository class in storage/repository.py. Any other module is forbidden from directly calling open() on a data file.' report_export.py:18 calls open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") directly to write data/report.json, bypassing Repository entirely.
findings.why_tests_missed_it: test_report_export.py exercises the real export_report code path end-to-end but never checks whether persistence goes through Repository — it only inspects the file contents after the fact via its own open() call. No test asserts that Repository.save() was called; the architectural constraint is not encoded in any test.
findings.fix: Add a save_report(name, payload) method (or reuse save()) on Repository, then have export_report accept an optional repo parameter and call repo.save(...) instead of open(). Update the test to either pass a FakeRepo or assert Repository.save is invoked.
